I think it would be rash to expect FYCOMPA to survive significant generic competition intact. Catalyst could easily experience significant erosions in its FYCOMPA revenues in 2026. Investors should consider this as a known unknown.

AGAMREE

While FYCOMPA lacks IP protection AGAMREE has comparably robust IP protection as shown by Presentation slide 12 below:

It is however far from ironclad, particularly after 04/2031. The 10-Q describes it in greater detail as:

In the U.S., AGAMREE® has New Chemical Entity exclusivity that expires in October 2028. AGAMREE® also has Orphan Drug Exclusivity expiring in October 2030. AGAMREE® is further protected by seven Orange Book listed patents expiring as early as May 28, 2029 and as late as July 16, 2040. The Company has also requested Patent Term Extension and will update the relevant expiration date in the Orange Book upon a final determination by the USPTO. The earliest a generic manufacturer could submit an ANDA is October 26, 2027. If we were to pursue a patent infringement action if any such ANDA challenges any of AGAMREE®’s Orange Book patents, then the automatic statutory 30-month stay would prevent FDA approval of such ANDA until April 26, 2031. [italics added]

Presentation slide 13 below shows the AGAMREE market opportunity:

This slide is not clear as to what it means by total addressable market for DMD population of >$1 Billion as regards AGAMREE. The footnote clarifies that it is not its 2025 guidance. During the Call CEO Daly gave the following 2025 AGAMREE guidance:

... early commercial indicators position AGAMREE for broader market reach as foundational complementary corticosteroid therapy within the evolving DMD treatment landscape. This momentum continues to support our full year 2025 net revenue forecast for AGAMREE between $100 million and $110 million.

This is certainly a positive indication for a therapy's second full year on the market. AGAMREE totaled ~$47 million in net sales from its 03/2024 launch through 12/31/2024. If you add its $22 million in Q1, 2025 revenues to this it comes to $69 million for its first full year revenues.

If it achieves its guided midpoint of $105 million for 2025 it will represent AGAMREE growth ~$36 million far above the expected FIRDAPSE growth.

Combined performance

Catalyst achieved strong top and bottom line beats for Q1, 2025. Its overall revenue of $141 million bettered analysts' expectations by $9.87 million. Its bottom line non-GAAP EPS of $0.68 beat expectations by $0.15.

Slide 23 below shows the relative growth dynamic among Catalyst's three approved products. FYCOMPA is expected to fall back after being leapfrogged by AGAMREE thanks to AGAMREE's superior IP protections:

Catalyst's superior financial profile earns it wide analyst support.

Presentation slide 20 below provides a concise overview of Catalyst's enviable financial profile:

Its year over year Q1 growth comparisons are skewed by the fact that its highest growing product AGAMREE was not fully launched until Q2, 2024. The slide is helpful in its report of $580.7 million cash and cash equivalents with no debt as of 03/31/2025.It is also significant in that it has positive Earnings Per Share Diluted (GAAP) of $0.19 for Q1, 2024 and $0.45 for Q1, 2025.

Its positive financial profile earns it a rare, for small pharmas, "Buy" recommendation from Seeking Alpha's metrics based quant system. Indeed the quant system's Ratings Summary PANEL is green across the board:

Wall Street analysts are particularly enthused by Catalyst's prospects. Seven of the eight Wall Street analysts covering the stock rate it as a "Strong Buy". The average price target for the eight analysts is $34 for an ~38% upside.

Summary

I rate Catalyst as a "Strong Buy". Its buy and build business model is proving to be highly effective. Its steady FIRDAPSE growth coupled with its more aggressive AGAMREE growth make for an attractive counter to its flagging FYCOMPA revenues.

Risks to this "Strong Buy" thesis are not hard to find. Most serious are those founded on IP issues. I have already discussed FYCOMPA's unfortunate IP vulnerability.

Its lead product FIRDAPSE has long been beset by competitive IP challenges. Catalyst has been successful in knocking these aside so far, however as reported in the 10-Q additional FIRDAPSE IP challenges keep popping up with several IP lawsuits pending.

AGAMREE is not immune from IP concerns despite its noted protection. Catalyst's latest 10-K (p. 18) indicates that patients transitioning to Sarepta’s (SRPT) Elevidys recently approved DMD gene therapy are likely to interrupt or postpone AGAMREE treatment. I do not expect this to be a scalable concern however it does illustrate the potential for unexpected IP hurdles.
